what: Combine the v183 bs512/E257 NT=False win with the v179 sparse bs16/E257 block_m=32 path on top of the v176 bundle
why: Benchmark data says v183 is the current best bundle at 121.66us, and bs16/E257 block_m=32 improved the same sparse family in the neighboring branch; the combined sparse-shape tweak has not been benchmarked together yet.

what: Resubmit the exact v183 bundle for leaderboard confirmation
why: v183 is the strongest benchmark-backed candidate on this branch at 121.66us, materially ahead of the stored leaderboard best 125.64us, so it is the right scarce leaderboard spend while v186 is still unproven.

what: Pre-inject fused_moe config monkeypatches at module scope on top of the v183 bundle
why: The exact v183 kernel mix is already the best benchmarked candidate, so the next low-risk experiment is trimming Python dispatch overhead by doing config injection once at import time instead of on every custom_kernel call. A prior combined attempt also changed inference_mode; this isolates only the pre-injection change.

what: Monkeypatch get_2stage_cfgs to force cktile_moe block_m=32 for sparse E=257 shapes (bs16 and bs128) on top of the v183 bundle
why: Prior E257 block_m experiments only wrote block_m into cfg_2stages, and the historical notes say cktile_moe ignores that field unless metadata is patched directly. This is the first real test of larger cktile sorting granularity for the sparse E257 shapes while keeping the strong v183 bs512 path unchanged.

what: Patch cktile metadata to force block_m=32 only for bs16/E257 on top of the v183 bundle
why: v189 changes both sparse cktile E257 shapes at once. This isolate keeps the strong v183 bs128 and bs512 choices intact and tests whether the tiny bs16 sparse shape alone benefits when block_m is overridden through returned metadata rather than ignored cfg entries.

what: Correct the sparse-metadata isolate to preserve the v183 bs128/E257 ksplit=2 path while testing only the bs16 block_m metadata override
why: The working tree for v189/v190 accidentally inherited the old bs128 ksplit=1 regression from v185. The right isolate is the exact v183 bundle plus the bs16 metadata patch, otherwise any result is confounded by the known-bad bs128 setting.

what: Port the later V193 mode-adaptive dispatch policy from the sibling amd-moe-mxfp4 workspace
why: The local v183-style NT and metadata tweaks are now disproven on both BM and LB. The sibling workspace converged on a different E257 policy: BYPASS+KSPLIT=7 for E257 M<=128, KSPLIT=1 for E257 M>128, plus cached sorting/stage1 and the known E33 paths. That is the only remaining concrete candidate backed by later hardware evidence.

what: Force block_size_M=64 for E=257 medium CK Tile shapes (M<=128) on top of the V193-style mode-adaptive dispatch
why: The later sibling amd-moe-mxfp4 search reached a dedicated V224 probe for this exact branch. Local history only rejected block_m=64 on older sparse/NT bundles, not on the BYPASS+KSPLIT=7 medium-shape CK Tile path.

what: Remove the Python-side sorting and CK Tile stage1 buffer caches while keeping the current mode-adaptive dispatch policy
why: The sibling amd-moe-mxfp4 tree carried a dedicated clean-baseline probe for this question, and this repo has not benchmarked a no-cache variant. If the current leaderboard path is sensitive to allocator or cache-pollution effects, the simplest way to expose it is to keep the dispatch choices and delete only the Python reuse layers.

what: Remove AITER_USE_OPUS_MOE_SORTING from the current mixed-dispatch kernel
why: Sibling analysis found OPUS sorting to be neutral for the CK Tile path and the earlier local OPUS monkeypatch result was only noise. This repo has not benchmarked the clean mixed-dispatch kernel without the env var, so it remains a valid one-line isolate for the shared sorting path.

what: Use CSV/default for E=257 M>128 while keeping the E=257 M<=128 CK Tile block_m=64 probe
why: The sibling amd-moe-mxfp4 v224 branch only tested the medium-shape E257 CK Tile block_m=64 path and left large E257 on CSV/default. That is materially different from our current ksplit=1 large-E257 path and is more leaderboard-plausible after the v187 revert disproved the aggressive all-E257 CK Tile branch.

what: Port the sibling v224 policy exactly: cached buffers plus OPUS env, with E257 medium on CK Tile block_m=64 and large E257 on CSV/default
why: v196 matches the sibling shape split but still differs materially by stripping the cached sorting/stage1 hooks and OPUS env var. The sibling v224 probe kept those pieces, so this exact port is still untested in this repo and is the cleanest remaining variant of the only later evidence-backed branch.

what: Use CK Tile ksplit=3 with block_size_M=32 for large E257 shapes while keeping the cached medium-E257 block_m=64 branch
why: Sibling server sweeps found k=3 best for E257 M=512 and later notes say CK Tile split-k paths want block_size_M=32. This repo has rejected k=2 and CSV/default variants for bs512/E257, but not a clean cached k=3+bsm32 branch on top of the current medium-E257 policy.

what: Use CK Tile ksplit=2 with block_size_M=32 for large E257 shapes while keeping the cached medium-E257 block_m=64 branch
why: The cached k=1 branch is already covered by v193 and the cached k=3 branch is now covered by v198. The sibling workspace also carried a dedicated large-E257 k=2 sweep with bsm=32, which remains locally unqueued and is the last clean neighboring branch in this family.

what: Port the sibling e257_ksweep policy exactly: E257 medium k7 with auto block_size_M, E257 large k2 with block_size_M=32
why: v193/v197/v198/v199 all force block_size_M=64 for the medium E257 CK Tile path. The sibling workspace also carried a later exact e257_ksweep variant that kept medium E257 on auto block_size_M while sweeping only the large-E257 split-k choice. That exact cached branch remains unqueued here.

what: Use CSV/default for large E257 while keeping cached medium E257 on auto block_size_M with KSPLIT=7
why: v197 covered large-E257 CSV with medium forced to block_m=64, and v200 covered medium auto with large-E257 ksplit=2. The remaining clean control in this family is medium auto plus large CSV, which matches the medium path of the sibling e257_ksweep branch without forcing the large CK Tile backend.

what: Use CK Tile ksplit=1 for large E257 shapes while keeping cached medium E257 on auto block_size_M with KSPLIT=7
why: v193 covered large-E257 ksplit=1 with medium forced to block_m=64, and v200/v201 covered the medium-auto controls for large-E257 ksplit=2 and CSV. The last clean hole in this family is medium-auto plus large-E257 ksplit=1.

what: Use CK Tile ksplit=3 for large E257 shapes while keeping cached medium E257 on auto block_size_M with KSPLIT=7
why: v198 covered large-E257 ksplit=3 with medium forced to block_m=64, and v200/v201/v202 now cover the medium-auto controls for large-E257 ksplit=2, CSV, and ksplit=1. The final clean hole in this family is medium-auto plus large-E257 ksplit=3.
